free_text: Study of isomeric ratios of proton-nucleus cross
sections on tin isotopes
Armenian State University.
English translation of YF,59,592
HPGe semiconductor detector (with a resolution
of 1.9 keV for the 1332.5-keV line of 60Co) and a
4096-channel amplitude analyzer.
phasotron of the JINR (Dubna).
measurement of gamma-activity were
started after 1 hour for Sn-124, 1.5 hour for Sn-118
and after 4 hour for Sn-112 targets and were
repeatedly measured over a period of one year.
Dependences of the isomer ratios of the cross sections
and of the behavior of the high-spin and low-spin
states on the number of emitted protons are obtained
for several residual nuclei.
Some decay data were used by authors are incorrect.
All uncertainties are statistical.
Tabs 1,2
English translation of 'YF' was added
SD: Corrections in all Subents. MONITOR
added. SAMPLE moved to Subents 002-138. All values
updated according to presentation in the Tables.
EN moved to Subent 001 and E=600. -> 660. MeV.
SD: Corrections in Subents 124-126.
Enrichment Sn-112 - 92.6%.
Sn-112 target has thickness 390 mg/cm**2.

METHOD deleted (no info in the article).

Three layers of Sn-124 foils of cross-sectional area
2*2 cm2 were used. The thickness of one layer was
- By Compiler - Isomer state of Sn-123 could be
produced due to radioactive decay of In-123, which
could be produced by reaction Sn-124(P,2P)In-123.
Authors did not explaine the reasons for excluding
(p,2p) reaction.
